description: Review of linear transformations, duality. Diagonalization of linear transformations.The Cayley Hamilton Theorem. Minimal polynomials. The Jordan canonical form. Exponential function. Inner product spaces, orthonormal bases and Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization. Operators on inner product spaces. Self-adjoint, unitary and positive operators. Spectral theorem. singular value decomposition. Bilinear and quadratic forms. Norms, bounded linear operators, matrix norms. Basic multilinear algebra. This is a second course in linear algebra.

optimized_prerequisites.prerequisites_text: (MATH 234or375), (MATH 320,340,341, or375), and (MATH 341,375,421,467, or521), or graduate/professional standing or member of the Pre-Masters Mathematics (Visiting International) Program
